meaning: adj.St.Francis的,圣芳济会的n.圣芳济会的修道士
examples: Mitch was a San Franciscan by birth.
The first elementary school in the Americas was set up in Mexico in 1523 by a Franciscan monk.
Saint Francis of Assisi renounced an earthly fortune, founded the Franciscan order of monks and preached to birds.
However, it was young, valiant San Franciscan engineer, William Hammond Hall, who took on the 20-year project.
It is hard to think of a single important Franciscan principle that was not violated.
